title: Yoshua Bengio Interview. Also see: AI Safety Summit with Yoshua… | by Kevin O'Shaughnessy | Jan, 2026 | Medium
excerpts:
Section Title: Yoshua Bengio Interview
Content:
Bengio explains the Bayesian Posture: if there are multiple ways to interpret the data we have about the world which are all consistent with the data, then bring all of those points of view into the decision that you’re going to take. He advocates embracing other people’s views.
So we see that, although he’s won a Turing award and often described as one of the “Godfathers of AI”, he remains humble, just as scientific principles demand.
Bengio mentions that Yann LeCun has a different position on Open Source LLMs. He explains that Open Source has benefits, but also dangers, and that decisions of when to Open Source technology should be based on how dangerous it is. He gives the analogy of learning to swim — you should still get in the water, with appropriate supervision, but maybe not jump off the high board into the deep end just yet, and not into the ocean in the middle of a storm.

title: Yoshua Bengio on why AI can behave unpredictably (and ...
excerpts:
Section Title: ... > [Kate Whiting](https://www.weforum.org/stories/authors/kate-whiting/)
Content:
Senior Writer, Forum Stories
Yoshua Bengio is a Professor of Computer Science at the University of Montreal and one of the 'Godfathers of AI' alongside his mentors Geoffrey Hinton and Yann LeCun.
At the World Economic Forum Annual Meeting in January, he sat down with Radio Davos to explain why AI can behave badly – and his solution.
In this edited interview, Bengio explains the lightbulb moment that spurred him to work on an AI system with no hidden agenda.
In early 2023, like many people, Yoshua Bengio was experimenting with ChatGPT. The Canadian computer scientist is widely considered one of the 'Godfathers of AI' for his pioneering work on neural networks and deep learning, so when he engages with these systems, he does so with a deep understanding of how they work.
